answerBox: Rent Finder Cleveland's rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland proper, a few miles from South Euclid in neighborhoods like Glenville and Hough. We don't currently work with homes inside South Euclid itself, but it's notable as one of the few Cuyahoga County suburbs with a local ordinance protecting Section 8 voucher holders.

sections.h2: Renting a house in South Euclid, Ohio
body: South Euclid is a Cuyahoga County suburb in the northeast part of the region, bordering Cleveland Heights and East Cleveland. It sits a few miles from Cleveland's Glenville and Hough neighborhoods and from University Circle, and is close to the **RTA Red Line's Windermere terminus** in neighboring East Cleveland.

Rent Finder Cleveland works with Section 8-friendly rental homes in Cleveland's Glenville (44108) and Hough (44103) areas nearby, but we don't currently have listings inside South Euclid itself. If South Euclid is where you want to rent, [tell our team what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) — we can flag comparable homes close by and update you if that changes.

South Euclid draws interest from renters searching for both Cleveland Heights-adjacent housing and the wider University Circle job market. Our honest answer is that our current inventory sits a few miles away in Cleveland proper, but given South Euclid's voucher protections, it's worth telling us what you need so we can flag options nearby as they open up.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent a house near South Euclid?
body: South Euclid doesn't publish its own rent index, so the most useful reference point is the broader Cleveland-metro market. Zumper's median asking rent (report dated July 4, 2026) and RentCafe's average rent for professionally managed apartments (report dated July 2, 2026) are two dated, commonly cited sources for the region as a whole — treat them as general context rather than a South Euclid-specific figure.

The two differ in method: RentCafe's number leans toward larger, professionally managed apartment communities, while Zumper's median spans the full range of listings across the metro, including older housing stock more typical of inner-ring suburbs like South Euclid.

South Euclid is close to the **RTA Red Line's** Windermere terminus in neighboring East Cleveland, and GCRTA's regular bus network covers the suburb directly, connecting to Tower City, University Circle, and the HealthLine on Euclid Avenue. Renters commuting to Cleveland Clinic, University Hospitals, or Case Western Reserve University in University Circle have several transit options without needing a car for the full trip.

As a northeast-side suburb, South Euclid also falls within Cuyahoga County's higher-snowfall belt compared with Cleveland's west side and lakefront communities. Renters relocating from milder climates should plan for a full snow season, typically November through January, before lake-effect snow tapers off with Lake Erie's freeze.

sections.h2: Section 8 and voucher protections in South Euclid
body: South Euclid stands out among Cuyahoga County suburbs: as of July 2026, it is one of only five county municipalities — alongside Cleveland Heights, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale — reported to have a local ordinance protecting Section 8 (Housing Choice Voucher) holders from source-of-income discrimination. Ohio has no such protection at the state level, and the City of Cleveland has not enacted one either, so this local rule is a meaningful distinction for voucher holders searching here.

The **Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)** administers the voucher program countywide and accepts [preliminary applications year-round through its online portal](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php), with placement by periodic lottery. Applying requires an active email address, Social Security numbers and birth dates for the household, and income details; CMHA's income limits (effective April 2025) run from $34,800 for one person up to $65,650 for an eight-person household, updated annually by HUD.

Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, including nearby homes in Glenville and Hough. See our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full application process.

sections.h2: Reach out about renting near South Euclid
body: If South Euclid is on your list but you're open to nearby options, take a look at [rental homes in Glenville](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-glenville-cleveland/) or [Hough](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-hough-cleveland/), both a short drive away. [Contact our team](/p/book-showing) or call (440) 444-4737 — we'll let you know about voucher-friendly homes near South Euclid as they become available.

Since our availability shifts regularly, the most reliable next step is to tell us directly what you need — bedroom count, budget, and whether you're using a Housing Choice Voucher — so we can match you with current or upcoming homes in Glenville and Hough, both within a short drive of South Euclid.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house near South Euclid?
faq.a: South Euclid doesn't publish its own rent figures, but Cleveland-metro benchmarks provide context: Zumper's July 2026 report shows a median rent near $1,250 a month, while RentCafe's average for professionally managed apartments is $1,564. Actual rents near South Euclid depend on the home's size, age, and condition.

faq.q: Does South Euclid protect Section 8 voucher holders?
faq.a: Yes, reportedly. As of July 2026, South Euclid is one of five Cuyahoga County municipalities with a local ordinance protecting voucher holders from source-of-income discrimination, alongside Cleveland Heights, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale. Ohio has no such statewide protection, and Cleveland proper has not enacted one.

faq.q: Is South Euclid served by public transit?
faq.a: South Euclid is near the RTA Red Line's Windermere terminus in neighboring East Cleveland and is covered by GCRTA's regular bus network, which connects to Tower City, University Circle, and the HealthLine on Euclid Avenue.
